15	// ReviewGroup reviews the same bead/result_rev with two reviewer slots.
16	// The helper shares one evidence bundle and prompt file across both slots and
17	// returns the structured reviewer results alongside the shared bundle
18	// metadata. It does not change execute-loop close/block behavior.
19	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {
20		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
21		if err != nil {
22			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
23		}
24	
25		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
26		if err != nil {
27			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
28		}
29	
30		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
31		iter := 1
32	
33		caps := r.Caps
34		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
35			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
36		}
37		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
38		groupID := GenerateAttemptID()
39		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, groupID)
40		if err != nil {
41			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
42		}
43		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(built.Prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
45		}
46	
47		reviewHarness := r.Harness
48		if reviewHarness == "" {
49			reviewHarness = impl.Harness
50		}
51		reviewModel := r.Model
52		if reviewModel == "" && reviewHarness != "" {
53			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
54		}
55	
56		out := &ReviewGroupResult{
57			BeadID:    beadID,
58			ResultRev: resultRev,
59			Bundle: ReviewGroupBundle{
60				GroupID:   groupID,
61				DirAbs:    artifacts.DirAbs,
62				DirRel:    artifacts.DirRel,
63				PromptAbs: artifacts.PromptAbs,
64				PromptRel: artifacts.PromptRel,
65			},
66			Slots: make([]ReviewGroupSlotResult, 0, 2),
67		}
68	
69		for reviewerIndex := 0; reviewerIndex < 2; reviewerIndex++ {
70			slotRuntime := BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewModel, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{
71				GroupID:       groupID,
72				ReviewerIndex: reviewerIndex,
73			})
74			slotRuntime.PromptFile = artifacts.PromptAbs
75			slotRuntime.WorkDir = r.ProjectRoot
76	
77			slotResult, slotErr := r.reviewGroupSlot(ctx, b, impl, resultRev, built, artifacts, reviewHarness, reviewModel, slotRuntime, caps.MaxPromptBytes)
78			slot := ReviewGroupSlotResult{
79				ReviewerIndex: reviewerIndex,
80				Runtime:       slotRuntime,
81			}
82			if slotResult != nil {
83				slot.Result = slotResult
84			}
85			if slotErr != nil {
86				slot.Error = slotErr.Error()
87			}
88			out.Slots = append(out.Slots, slot)
89			if slotErr != nil {
90				return out, slotErr
91			}
92		}
93	
94		return out, nil
95	}

98		prompt := built.Prompt

111			}, fmt.Errorf("review-group: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)", len(prompt), maxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
112		}
113	
114		start := time.Now()
115		runRuntime := runtime
116		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
117		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)

120		if runErr != nil {

132				InputBytes:      len(prompt),
133				OutputBytes:     0,
134			}
135			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("review-group: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
136		}
137	
138		actualHarness := reviewHarness
139		actualModel := reviewModel
140		actualProvider := ""
141		actualPower := 0
142		if result != nil {
143			if result.Harness != "" {
144				actualHarness = result.Harness
145			}
146			if result.Model != "" {
147				actualModel = result.Model
148			}
149			actualProvider = result.Provider
150			actualPower = result.ActualPower
151			durationMS = result.DurationMS
152		}
153	
154		output := ""
155		sessionID := ""
156		if result != nil {
157			output = result.Output
158			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
159		}
160		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
161		var strictVerdict Verdict
162		var rationale string
163		var findings []Finding
164		if parseErr == nil {
165			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
166			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
167			findings = parsed.Findings
168			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
169				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
170				for _, f := range findings {
171					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
172					if line == "" {
173						continue
174					}
175					if f.Location != "" {
176						line = f.Location + ": " + line
177					}
178					parts = append(parts, line)
179				}
180				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
181			}
182		}
183		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
184		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
185			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
186			Rationale:        rationale,
187			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
188			RawOutput:        output,
189			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
190			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
191			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
192			SessionID:        sessionID,
193			BaseRev:          baseRev,
194			ResultRev:        resultRev,
195			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
196			DurationMS:       durationMS,
197			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
198			InputBytes:       len(prompt),
199			OutputBytes:      len(output),
200		}
201	
202		if parseErr != nil {
203			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
204			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
205				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
206			}
207			reviewRes.Error = class
208			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("review-group: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
209		}
210		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
211			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
212				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
213				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
214				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
215				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
216				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
217			})
218		}
219		return reviewRes, nil
220	}

14	// TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress verifies that each
15	// terminal review class (spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large,
16	// unsafe_or_out_of_scope) is correctly classified as a terminal operator-
17	// required outcome. Key assertions:
18	//   - Report status is ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock.
19	//   - Bead carries the needs_human label.
20	//   - A review-terminal-block event is appended with the correct class.
21	//   - isValidImplementationAttempt returns false → no no-progress budget consumed.
22	//   - Bead is NOT closed.
23	func TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress(t *testing.T) {
24		classes := []struct {
25			name  string
26			class string
27		}{
28			{"spec_gap", ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap},
29			{"missing_acceptance", ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance},
30			{"too_large", ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge},
31			{"unsafe_or_out_of_scope", ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope},
32		}
33		for _, tc := range classes {
34			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
35				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
36				worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
37					Store: store,
38					Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
39						return ExecuteBeadReport{
40							BeadID:    beadID,
41							Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
42							SessionID: "sess-terminal-" + tc.name,
43							ResultRev: "aabbcc01",
44							BaseRev:   "aabbcc00",
45						}, nil
46					}),
47					Reviewer: beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
48						return &ReviewResult{
49							Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
50							Rationale:       "This BLOCK is terminal: " + tc.class,
51							RawOutput:       "BLOCK: " + tc.class,
52							ReviewerHarness: "claude",
53							ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
54						}, nil
55					}),
56				}
57	
58				cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
59				rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
60				result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
61				require.NoError(t, err)
62	
63				// Must count as a failure, not a success.
64				assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
65				assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
66				assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock, result.LastFailureStatus)
67	
68				// Bead must remain open (not closed).
69				got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
70				require.NoError(t, err)
71				assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusInProgress, got.Status, "terminal block must not close the bead")
72	
73				// Bead must carry the needs_human label.
74				assert.True(t, HasBeadLabel(got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel),
75					"terminal block must add needs_human label (class=%s)", tc.class)
76	
77				// review-terminal-block event must be appended with the correct class.
78				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
79				require.NoError(t, err)
80				foundTerminal := false
81				for _, ev := range events {
82					if ev.Kind == ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind && ev.Summary == tc.class {
83						foundTerminal = true
84						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "terminal_class="+tc.class)
85						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "result_rev=aabbcc01")
86					}
87				}
88				assert.True(t, foundTerminal,
89					"expected review-terminal-block event with class %s", tc.class)
90	
91				// isValidImplementationAttempt must return false → no no-progress budget.
92				assert.False(t, isValidImplementationAttempt(ExecuteBeadReport{
93					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock,
94					BaseRev:   "aabbcc00",
95					ResultRev: "aabbcc01",
96				}), "review_terminal_block must not consume the no-progress retry budget")
97			})
98		}
99	}

101	// TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorNeedsHuman verifies that
102	// when review-error events exhaust the retry budget, the bead receives the
103	// needs_human label alongside the terminal review-manual-required event. This
104	// ensures exhausted review errors park the bead with operator-required metadata
105	// rather than silently cycling.
106	func TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorNeedsHuman(t *testing.T) {
107		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
108		const resultRev = "cafebabe"
109	
110		// Seed N-1 prior failures (default N=3 → 2 prior events). The next
111		// reviewer attempt becomes the 3rd, which must trip the terminal event.
112		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, resultRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty, 2)
113	
114		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
115			Store: store,
116			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
117				return ExecuteBeadReport{
118					BeadID:    beadID,
119					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
120					SessionID: "sess-exhausted-terminal",
121					ResultRev: resultRev,
122				}, nil
123			}),
124			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty),
125		}
126	
127		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
128		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
129		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131	
132		// review-manual-required event must be present with the correct class.
133		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
134		require.NoError(t, err)
135		var manual *bead.BeadEvent
136		for i := range events {
137			if events[i].Kind == "review-manual-required" {
138				manual = &events[i]
139			}
140		}
141		require.NotNil(t, manual, "exhausted review_error must emit a review-manual-required event")
142		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
143			"review-manual-required body must carry failure_class")
144		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "attempt_count=3",
145			"review-manual-required body must carry attempt_count")
146		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
147			"review-manual-required body must carry result_rev")
148	
149		// needs_human label must be added on exhausted review error.
150		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
151		require.NoError(t, err)
152		assert.True(t, HasBeadLabel(got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel),
153			"exhausted review_error must add needs_human label so the bead is surfaced for operator triage")
154	
155		// Bead must NOT be closed by reviewer-failure escalation.
156		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
157			"reviewer failure (even at terminal escalation) must never close the bead")
158	}

17	// TestReviewErrorTaxonomy covers FEAT-022 §12 / Stage G AC #1: each of the
18	// four review-error classes is emitted distinctly when its root cause fires,
19	// and the appended review-error event body carries the literal class
20	// identifier so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
21	func TestReviewErrorTaxonomy(t *testing.T) {
22		tests := []struct {
23			name      string
24			wantClass string
25			reviewer  beadReviewerFunc
26		}{
27			{
28				name:      "context_overflow",
29				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
30				// Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit returns ReviewResult.Error
31				// = OutcomeReviewContextOverflow alongside a wrapped error. The
32				// loop classifier reads the structured Error first.
33				reviewer: beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
34					return &ReviewResult{
35							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
36							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
37							ResultRev: resultRev,
38						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt 200000 bytes exceeds cap 100000)",
39							evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow)
40				}),
41			},
42			{
43				name:      "provider_empty",
44				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
45				reviewer: beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
46					return &ReviewResult{
47							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
48							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
49							ResultRev: resultRev,
50						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
51							ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
52				}),
53			},
54			{
55				name:      "unparseable",
56				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
57				reviewer: beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
58					return &ReviewResult{

13	// seedBlocks appends `n` review-BLOCK events spaced 1 minute apart, starting
14	// at base. Returns the timestamp of the last BLOCK so test code can layer
15	// pairing-degraded events around it.
16	func seedBlocks(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID string, base time.Time, n int) time.Time {
17		t.Helper()
18		last := base
19		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
20			last = base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Minute)
21			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
22				Kind:      "review",
23				Summary:   "BLOCK",
24				Body:      "rationale",
25				Actor:     "reviewer",
26				Source:    "test",
27				CreatedAt: last,
28			}))
29		}
30		return last
31	}

33	// findEvent returns the last event of kind `kind` for bead, or nil.
34	func findEvent(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, kind string) *bead.BeadEvent {

36		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
37		require.NoError(t, err)
38		for i := len(events) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
39			if events[i].Kind == kind {
40				ev := events[i]
41				return &ev
42			}
43		}
44		return nil
45	}

15	type ladderResponse struct {
16		next int
17		err  error
18	}
19	
20	type ladderSpy struct {
21		mu        sync.Mutex
22		calls     []int
23		responses []ladderResponse
24	}
25	
26	func (s *ladderSpy) Next(actualPower int) (int, error) {
27		s.mu.Lock()
28		defer s.mu.Unlock()
29		s.calls = append(s.calls, actualPower)
30		idx := len(s.calls) - 1
31		if idx >= len(s.responses) {
32			return 0, tierescalation.ErrLadderExhausted
33		}
34		resp := s.responses[idx]
35		return resp.next, resp.err
36	}
37	
38	func (s *ladderSpy) CalledWith() []int {
39		s.mu.Lock()
40		defer s.mu.Unlock()
41		out := make([]int, len(s.calls))
42		copy(out, s.calls)
43		return out
44	}

752	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
753	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
754	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
755	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
756	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
757	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {

759		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())

765		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{

823	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
824	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
825	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
826	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
827	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
828	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
829	//
830	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
831	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
832	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
833	// and stays bounded.
834	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
835		root := t.TempDir()
836		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
837		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
838		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
839	
840		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
841		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
842			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
843		}
844		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
845		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
846	
847		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
848		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
849		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
850		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
851		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
852			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
853		}
854		var bigLog strings.Builder
855		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
856			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
857		}
858		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
859		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
860			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
861		}
862	
863		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
864		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
865			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
866		}
867	
868		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
869		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
870		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
871		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
872	
873		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
874		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
875	
876		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
877		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
878		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
879		if err != nil {
880			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
881		}
882	
883		// Must NOT include the session log content.
884		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
885			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
886		}
887	
888		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
889		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
890			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
891		}
892		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
893			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
894		}
895	
896		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
897		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
898		if len(out) > 150_000 {
899			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
900		}
901	}

3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"

8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind is the event kind emitted when a BLOCK verdict
12	// is classified as terminal (requires human intervention, no automated retry).
13	const ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind = "review-terminal-block"
14	
15	// Terminal review class constants. A BLOCK review verdict whose rationale
16	// contains one of these labels is mapped to a terminal operator-required
17	// outcome instead of re-entering the automated retry cycle.
18	const (
19		ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap           = "review_spec_gap"
20		ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance = "review_missing_acceptance"
21		ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge          = "review_too_large"
22		ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope  = "review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope"
23	)
24	
25	// classifyTerminalReviewBlock returns the terminal class label if the BLOCK
26	// result's rationale signals a human-required condition, or "" if the BLOCK is
27	// a regular fixable gap that should re-enter the automated retry cycle.
28	//
29	// Detection is substring-based on the lowercased rationale so reviewer output
30	// only needs to include the class label anywhere in the text.
31	func classifyTerminalReviewBlock(res *ReviewResult) string {
32		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictBlock {
33			return ""
34		}
35		text := strings.ToLower(res.Rationale)
36		switch {
37		case strings.Contains(text, ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap):
38			return ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap
39		case strings.Contains(text, ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance):
40			return ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance
41		case strings.Contains(text, ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge):
42			return ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge
43		case strings.Contains(text, ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope):
44			return ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope
45		default:
46			return ""
47		}
48	}
49	
50	// reviewTerminalBlockEventBody builds the structured body for a
51	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind event, encoding the terminal class and
52	// result_rev so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
53	func reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev string) string {
54		return fmt.Sprintf("terminal_class=%s\nresult_rev=%s", class, resultRev)
55	}
56	
57	// applyTerminalReviewBlock records terminal block state on a bead: adds the
58	// needs_human label, appends a review-terminal-block event, and parks the
59	// bead under MaxLoopCooldown. All store errors are best-effort — callers
60	// continue regardless so a store-side failure cannot strand the bead.
61	func applyTerminalReviewBlock(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, class, resultRev string) {
62		_ = store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
63			if !HasBeadLabel(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel) {
64				b.Labels = append(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel)
65			}
66		})
67		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
68			Kind:      ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind,
69			Summary:   class,
70			Body:      reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev),
71			Actor:     actor,
72			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
73			CreatedAt: now.UTC(),
74		})
75		parkUntil := now.UTC().Add(CapLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
76		_ = store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, parkUntil, ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind, class)
77	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
10	
11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
24	
25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
41		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
42		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
43		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
44		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
45		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
46	)
47	
48	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
49	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
50	// queue.
51	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
52	
53	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
54	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
55	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
56	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
57	//
58	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
59	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
60	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
61	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
62	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
63	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
64		switch outcome {
65		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
66			if exitCode == 0 {
67				return ""
68			}
69			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
70			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
71		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
72			return FailureModeNoChanges
73		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
74			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
75		}
76	
77		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
78		switch {
79		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
80			return FailureModeLockContention
81		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
82			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
85			"passthrough constraint:",
86			"max_power is less than min_power",
87			"max_power exceeds min_power",
88			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
89			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
90			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
91			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
92			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
93			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint

177	// IsWorktreeLostError reports whether errMsg describes the execute-bead
178	// infrastructure losing the isolated attempt worktree before DDx could inspect
179	// its HEAD. This deliberately requires both a HEAD/worktree signal and a
180	// missing-path signal so generic harness "no such file" errors do not get
181	// bucketed as infrastructure loss.
182	func IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg string) bool {
183		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
184		hasHeadSignal := containsAny(lower,
185			"failed to read worktree head",
186			"git rev-parse head",
187			"worktree path missing")
188		hasMissingPathSignal := containsAny(lower,
189			"chdir",
190			"no such file or directory",
191			"cannot access",
192			"does not exist")
193		return hasHeadSignal && hasMissingPathSignal
194	}
195	
196	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
197	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
198	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
199		for _, n := range needles {
200			if n == "" {
201				continue
202			}
203			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
204				return true
205			}
206		}
207		return false
208	}
209	
210	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
211	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
212	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
213	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
214	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
215	// bead merged cleanly.
216	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
217		switch landingOutcome {
218		case "merged":
219			return ""
220		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
221			return FailureModeNoChanges
222		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
223			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
224		case "error":
225			if workerMode != "" {
226				return workerMode
227			}
228			return FailureModeUnknown
229		case "preserved":
230			switch landingReason {
231			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
232				return FailureModeMergeConflict
233			case "post-run checks failed":
234				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
235			case RatchetPreserveReason:
236				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
237			case OperatorCancelReason:
238				return ""
239			}
240			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
241			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
242			if workerMode != "" {
243				return workerMode
244			}
245			return FailureModeUnknown
246		}
247		if workerMode != "" {
248			return workerMode
249		}
250		return FailureModeUnknown
251	}
252	
253	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
254	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
255	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
256	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
257	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
258	// test-oriented by convention.
259	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
260		for _, gr := range gateResults {
261			if gr.Status != "fail" {
262				continue
263			}
264			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
265			if containsAny(combined,
266				"build failed",
267				"compilation failed",
268				"compile error",
269				"compilation error",
270				"cannot find package",
271				"undefined:",
272				"undefined reference",
273				"syntax error") {
274				return FailureModeBuildFailure
275			}
276		}
277		return FailureModeTestFailure
278	}
279	
280	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
281	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
282	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
283	const (
284		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
285		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
286		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
287		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
288	)
289	
290	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
291	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
292	const (
293		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
294		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
295		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
296		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
297		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
298		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
299		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
300		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
301		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
302		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
303		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
304		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
305		ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted          = "resource_exhausted"
306	
307		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
308		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
309		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
310		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
311		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
312		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
313		//
314		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
315		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
316		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
317		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
318		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
319		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
320	
321		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
322		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
323		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
324		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
325		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
326	
327		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
328		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
329		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
330		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
331		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
332	
333		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
334		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
335		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
336		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
337		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
338	
339		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
340		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
341		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
342		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
343		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
344		// budget; the bead is parked with a needs_human label until an operator
345		// intervenes.
346		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"
347	)
348	
349	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
350	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
351	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
352	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
353		if exitCode != 0 {
354			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
355		}
356	
357		switch outcome {
358		case "merged":
359			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
360			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
361			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
362			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
363			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
364			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
365				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
366			}
367			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
368				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
369			}
370			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
371		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
372			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
373		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
374			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
375		case "error":
376			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
377		case "preserved":
378			switch reason {
379			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
380				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
381			case "post-run checks failed":
382				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
383			case RatchetPreserveReason:
384				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
385			case OperatorCancelReason:
386				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
387			default:
388				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
389					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
390				}
391				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
392				// explicitly requested no merge.
393				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
394			}
395		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
396			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
397		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
398			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
399		default:
400			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
401		}
402	}
403	
404	// IsResourceExhaustedStatus reports whether the status is the stable
405	// host-resource failure classification.
406	func IsResourceExhaustedStatus(status string) bool {
407		return status == ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted
408	}
409	
410	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
411		for _, prefix := range []string{
412			"large-deletion gate:",
413			"syntax sanity gate:",
414			"post-land gate failed:",
415		} {
416			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
417				return true
418			}
419		}
420		return false
421	}
422	
423	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
424		switch {
425		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
426			return reason + ": " + errMsg
427		case reason != "":
428			return reason
429		case errMsg != "":
430			return errMsg
431		case status != "":
432			return status
433		default:
434			return "unknown"
435		}
436	}

23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.

80	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
81	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
82	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

84	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
85	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
86	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
87	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
88	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
89	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.

92	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
93	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
94	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
95	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads

113	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
114	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
115	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
116	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
117	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
118	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
119	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.

125	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
126	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
127	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
128	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer

130	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(

145		}

150			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park

179	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
180	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
181	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
182	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
183	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
184		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
185		if hook == nil {
186			return report
187		}
188		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
189		if err != nil {
190			if runtime.Log != nil {
191				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
192			}
193			return report
194		}
195		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
196		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
197		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
198		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
199			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
200		}
201		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
202			return report
203		}
204		if classificationRecognized {
205			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
206		}
207		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
208		return report
209	}

211	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
212	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
213	// path can silently park a bead for a year.

215		if d <= 0 {

217		}

219			return MaxLoopCooldown

463	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
464		if w.Store == nil {
465			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
466		}
467		if w.Executor == nil {
468			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
469		}
470	
471		now := w.Now
472		if now == nil {
473			now = time.Now
474		}
475		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
476		if assignee == "" {
477			assignee = "ddx"
478		}
479		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
480		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
481			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
482		}
483		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
484		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
485			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
486		}
487		harness := rcfg.Harness()
488		model := rcfg.Model()
489		profile := rcfg.Profile()
490	
491		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
492		resultsResetIdx := 0
493		complexityGuard := work.NewComplexityGuard(w.ComplexityGate, runtime.Log)
494		preclaimGuard := work.NewPreClaimGuard(runtime.PreClaimHook, w.Store, runtime.Log, now, 30*time.Second)
495	
496		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
497			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
498		}
499	
500		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
501			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
502			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
503			"harness":      harness,
504			"model":        model,
505			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
506			"assignee":     assignee,
507			"once":         runtime.Once,
508		})
509		cleanupLog := runtime.CleanupLog
510		if cleanupLog == nil {
511			cleanupLog = runtime.Log
512		}
513		cleanupStop := startExecutionCleanupWorker(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, runtime.CleanupInterval, runtime.CleanupTickCh, cleanupLog, emit)
514		defer cleanupStop()
515		_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "startup")
516		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
517		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Work-owned terminal reasons
518		// are classified through work.StopCondition; fatal_config,
519		// preflight_failed, resource_exhausted, and future provider exhaustion are
520		// still subsystem-specific exits.
521		exitReason := ""
522		applyStop := func(input work.StopInput) bool {
523			decision, ok := work.ClassifyStop(input)
524			if !ok {
525				return false
526			}
527			exitReason = decision.ExitReason
528			return true
529		}
530		if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
531			if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
532				detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
533					harness, model, rerr.Error())
534				if runtime.Log != nil {
535					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (startup)\n", detail)
536				}
537				emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
538					"harness": harness,
539					"model":   model,
540					"reason":  rerr.Error(),
541					"startup": true,
542				})
543				report := ExecuteBeadReport{
544					Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
545					Detail:           detail,
546					Harness:          harness,
547					Model:            model,
548					Disrupted:        true,
549					DisruptionReason: "preflight_rejected",
550					OutcomeReason:    "preflight_failed",
551				}
552				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, "", "preflight_rejected", detail, harness, model, assignee, now().UTC())
553				result.Failures++
554				result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status

558			}
559		}

563				"successes":           result.Successes,
564				"failures":            result.Failures,
